skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: During an 8-second time interval, the average acceleration a, in meters per second squared, of an object with an initial velocity of 20 meters per second is defined by the equation a = (v𝕗 − 20)/8, where v𝕗 is the final velocity of the object in meters per second. If the equation is rewritten in the form v𝕗 = 8a + k, what is the value of the constant k?
rationale: Multiplying both sides of a = (v𝕗 − 20)/8 by 8 yields 8a = v𝕗 − 20. Adding 20 to both sides: v𝕗 = 8a + 20. So k = 20.
